meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Stop Version Control Hell for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Use this SaaS starter kit checklist to solve version control issues with Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for intermediate freelancers.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracking files. By deploying a standardized SaaS starter kit, you enforce strict version control, eliminate environment drift, and automate deployment pipelines. Use Antigravity for architecture scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinements to ensure every client project is production-ready, versioned, and scalable from the very first commit.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a folder named 'final_v2_real_final' while your client asks for a feature you deleted three hours ago. The terror of no version control isn't just a technical debt—it’s a professional liability that burns your reputation and kills your velocity. Every hour spent hunting for lost logic is an hour you aren't billing.

But the chaos ends here. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from reactive fire-fighting to proactive shipping. We’re moving beyond manual file management into a world where your infrastructure is as professional as your code. Let’s build your bulletproof foundation.

steps.title: Initialize Repository Foundation
steps.description: Establish a robust Git-based architecture immediately. We eliminate drift by forcing all client projects into a standardized starter kit structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready SaaS starter kit structure including .gitignore, .env.example, and a structured src directory with modular components.

steps.title: Configure Automated Versioning
steps.description: Automate your commit history and version tags so you never manually track changes again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up semantic release configuration and automated changelog generation within the repository.

steps.title: Implement Environment Isolation
steps.description: Protect your production secrets and ensure consistent behavior across local, staging, and production environments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create environment-specific configuration files and validate them against schema-based environment variables.

steps.title: Automate Code Quality Gates
steps.description: Integrate pre-commit hooks to ensure no code enters the repo without passing linting and type-checking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure husky pre-commit hooks to run eslint and tsc before every commit.

steps.title: Deploy Continuous Integration
steps.description: Finalize your pipeline so that every push triggers a deployment, keeping your client updated automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tests and builds the project on every push to main.

pro_tips: Always use conventional commits (feat:, fix:, chore:) to make your Git history machine-readable for automated changelogs.
Store your .env.example file in the repo, but ignore the actual .env file; use a vault service like Doppler for client secrets.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for every new feature before you commit; it keeps the codebase self-documenting.
Treat your starter kit as a living product; update it every time you solve a complex bug for a client.
Automate your deployments to a staging URL (like Vercel or Netlify) so clients can see progress without you needing to 'send the files'.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, create a template repository on GitHub so you can spin up new client projects in under 60 seconds. Then, begin integrating your preferred ORM and Auth provider to finalize your custom stack.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting time on manual file management and start shipping like a senior engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
